summary: The CI run exited with code 1 after Jest assertions using toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot encountered thrown errors that trace into the Babel parse error creation code.
root_cause: Jest assertions using expect.toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot failed because Babel parse errors were thrown (stack traces include createBabelParseError), leading to the test run exiting with code 1.

evidence.reason: Shows Jest assertions using toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot are present in the failing stack traces, indicating a snapshot-based assertion was involved.
Indicates the thrown error originated from Babel parsing utilities, implicating a parse error during tests.
Further shows parser error creation on the stack, supporting that parser/Babel errors triggered the test assertions.
Confirms the job failed (non-zero exit) after the test-related errors appeared in the logs.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failing tests locally (run the Jest suite) to see which toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot assertions fail. Inspect the error produced by src/language-js/parse/utilities/create-babel-parse-error.js and src/common/parser-create-error.js. If the error output intentionally changed, update the snapshots with jest -u; otherwise fix the parser/Babel behavior so the thrown errors match the expected snapshots.
